step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the transcript: 1. MagellanTV content creators (UserConsumerWebMobile) drop raw video/audio files into the S3 Source Bucket. 2. AWS Step Functions orchestrates the translation pipeline. 3. First, individual speaker segments are translated using Amazon Translate. 4. Next, Amazon Bedrock runs idiom/slang detection, sentence shortening, and cultural checks. 5. Amazon Polly converts the translated text scripts back to speech audio. 6. Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) routes the speech and scripts to human translators for quality assurance. 7. The final output files (video, sound, subtitle CCs) are saved in the S3 Output Bucket. 8. CloudFormation is used to spin up parallel processing nodes dynamically.

graph.name: Mission Cloud: Generative AI Video Translation Pipeline with Bedrock
graph.notes: Mission Cloud built a cost-effective content translation pipeline for MagellanTV. MagellanTV uploads videos to S3, triggering Step Functions to orchestrate a serverless translation and refinement flow. Text is translated via Amazon Translate and processed by Bedrock (slang detection, sentence shortening, and cultural checks). Amazon Polly generates speech audio, which is verified by human editors using Amazon A2I before final assets are stored in S3. CDK and CloudFormation scale the workflow across parallel nodes.

nodes.name: Content Creator
nodes.notes: MagellanTV editors who upload documentaries and perform quality checks.

nodes.name: Source Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores raw documentary video and audio files.

nodes.name: Workflow Orchestrator
nodes.notes: Orchestrates the serverless steps of the translation pipeline.

nodes.name: Amazon Translate
nodes.notes: Performs initial translation of transcript segments.

nodes.name: Amazon Bedrock
nodes.notes: Executes GenAI-based slang detection, sentence shortening, and cultural validation.

nodes.name: Amazon Polly
nodes.notes: Converts final translated text into synthesized speech.

nodes.name: Amazon A2I
nodes.notes: Enables human-in-the-loop translation review and validation.

nodes.name: Output Bucket
nodes.notes: Stores finalized video, sound, and closed-caption subtitle files.

nodes.name: AWS CloudFormation
nodes.notes: Provisions and scales parallel nodes for processing large volume batches.

edges.notes: Uploads documentaries to the raw ingest bucket.
Pushes audio transcript text to Translate.
Pushes refined, shortened, and culturally-checked script to Polly.
Sends synthesized speech audio to A2I validation workflow.
Saves verified multilingual media files to S3 output.
